analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: In eval mode every BatchNorm is _native_batch_norm_legit_no_training: a pure affine y = x*scale + bias with constant per-channel scale/bias. Inductor keeps it as a standalone elementwise pass (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_norm_legit_no_training_hardtanh_*) that streams the full activation tensor from DRAM, applies the affine + ReLU6 clamp, and writes it back. These kernels are flatly DRAM-bandwidth-bound: the dominant kernel k_00070 (256ch, 16x256x56x56) shows dram__throughput 84.54% of peak while sm__throughput is only 24.55% and tensor cores are idle (0%). The affine constants are fixed at inference, so the entire read+write of the activation exists only to apply a multiply-add that can instead be folded into the preceding convolution's weight and bias, eliminating the whole standalone tensor round-trip.

transformation.description: Fold each eval-mode BatchNorm (_native_batch_norm_legit_no_training) into the weight and bias of its immediately preceding convolution. For a conv with weight W (and optional bias b) followed by BN with running_mean mu, running_var var, gamma, beta, eps: scale = gamma / sqrt(var+eps); W' = W * scale[:,None,None,None]; b' = (b - mu) * scale + beta. After folding, the BN node is deleted and its elementwise affine DRAM round-trip disappears; only the ReLU6 (hardtanh) remains and Inductor will epilogue-fuse the clamp onto the conv output, so the standalone BN+hardtanh kernels collapse. The folded scale/bias are registered as new constant buffers, so this pass must run AFTER any dtype promotion (none proposed here, so it runs first in the pipeline).
transformation.location: Every conv->BN pair in all three blocks (depthwise->BN and pointwise->BN; 6 unique BN sites, replayed across the 12 measured kernels).
fx_steps: if conv.target not in (torch.ops.aten.convolution.default, torch.ops.aten.conv2d.default): continue
if len(conv.users) != 1: continue  # BN must be the sole consumer to fold safely
gamma, beta, rm, rv, eps = _resolve_bn_constants(node)  # all constant in no_training
fused_w, fused_b = torch.nn.utils.fuse_conv_bn_weights(weight_t, bias_t, rm, rv, eps, gamma, beta)
transformation.code_hint: torch.nn.utils.fuse_conv_bn_weights(conv_w, conv_b, bn.running_mean, bn.running_var, bn.eps, bn.weight, bn.bias) returns the exact (fused_w, fused_b). Apply at aten level keyed on _native_batch_norm_legit_no_training (the no_training overload guarantees constant statistics).

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes the standalone BN affine read+write of every activation. The 6 unique BN sites each stream the full activation twice (read+write); the largest writes ~182 KB/kernel at 84% of DRAM peak. The residual hardtanh clamp becomes a conv epilogue (no extra round-trip). Net: roughly one full activation read+write per block eliminated.

optimizations.notes: Folding is exact and lossless in eval mode (no_training BN uses fixed running stats). hardtanh (ReLU6) does NOT fold into weights but is a cheap clamp Inductor fuses into the conv epilogue. Highest-value change: BN+hardtanh is ~30% of attributed time and is pure wasted bandwidth.

bottleneck.description: The pointwise 1x1 convolutions lower to cutlass tensorop GEMMs (cutlass_80_tensorop_s1688gemm_*_tn_align4) and are the single most expensive nodes (op26=39423ns, op53=39584ns). They run on tensor cores but at low utilization: tensor_core_active_pct only 38-45%, achieved_occupancy 8.2-8.4% (wave starvation: grid is 784x1x1 CTAs against a Blackwell-class SM count, so each SM gets very few blocks), and eligible_cycles_pct 16-20% which on Blackwell (warp_cycles_per_instruction removed) flags a strongly latency-bound kernel. The '_tn' (transpose) layout plus the 1x1 spatial shape indicate the GEMM consumes an NCHW activation that must be implicitly transposed/gathered into a tensor-core-friendly layout (l1tex sector hit rate is 0% on these GEMMs). The kernel is dominated by memory-latency stalls feeding under-occupied tensor cores, not by tensor-core math throughput.

transformation.description: Annotate the convolution activations as channels_last (NHWC) so the 1x1-conv-as-GEMM consumes a layout where the channel (contraction) dimension is contiguous. A 1x1 conv is a per-pixel matmul over channels; with NCHW the contraction stride is H*W, forcing strided/transposed loads (the '_tn' path) and zero L1 reuse. In channels_last the C axis is innermost stride-1, letting cuDNN/cutlass pick an aligned tile with coalesced loads and better tensor-core feeding. The depthwise 3x3 convs and the BN/ReLU6 epilogues also prefer NHWC (depthwise is a per-channel spatial filter; NHWC keeps stride-1 channel access for the fused elementwise stages). Apply by inserting one aten.to(memory_format=channels_last) at the graph input and tagging conv weights channels_last, letting the format propagate through the conv/BN/relu chain so no per-op transpose kernels are inserted.
transformation.location: Network input and all three depthwise-separable blocks; weights of all 6 convolutions tagged channels_last; format propagates through conv -> (folded BN) -> hardtanh -> conv.
fx_steps: ph = next(n for n in gm.graph.nodes if n.op == 'placeholder')
# Verify no intervening aten._to_copy transpose kernels were inserted between conv/BN/relu stages.
transformation.code_hint: model = model.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last); inputs = inputs.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last). In the FX backend propagate channels_last through the conv stack and confirm Inductor does not emit per-op transpose kernels (a stray transpose reintroduces a DRAM round-trip and negates the gain).

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Converts strided/transposed activation loads in the 1x1 GEMMs to coalesced contiguous channel reads, improving tensor-core feed and L2 reuse. Estimated ~25-35% wall reduction across the six cutlass GEMM kernels (currently 146303 ns combined).

optimizations.notes: Bounded because these GEMMs are also wave-starved (occupancy ~8%): channels_last fixes the load pattern but the small 784-block grid at batch 16 / 56x56 still under-fills a Blackwell GPU. Confidence capped at medium (no doc/web tool available to confirm the exact cuDNN algo switch on this specific part). Must verify Inductor inserts no per-op transpose kernels.

bottleneck.description: The depthwise 3x3 convolutions (groups=C_in) are memory- and launch-bound: emitted either as a standalone Triton kernel (triton_poi_fused_convolution_0: dram__throughput ~51%, sm__throughput only ~10%, tensor cores idle, eligible_cycles_pct 8.6% -> severely latency-bound) or as a small conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc kernel. Depthwise conv does almost no arithmetic per loaded byte (one 3x3 filter per channel), so it is fundamentally bandwidth/launch-overhead limited. After OPT-1 folds BN away, the only thing separating each depthwise conv from its ReLU6 clamp is a kernel-launch boundary. Keeping the clamp as a separate kernel wastes a full activation read+write per boundary; fusing the clamp into the conv epilogue removes that intermediate DRAM materialization.

transformation.description: Fuse the ReLU6 (hardtanh, clamp[0,6]) activation into the epilogue of its producing convolution so no standalone elementwise kernel reads and rewrites the conv output. Combined with OPT-1's BN fold, each conv->BN->ReLU6 triple collapses to a single conv kernel with a (scale,bias,clamp) epilogue. This keys on the shared high-level pattern conv-output -> hardtanh, which at the functional/pre-decomposition level is one conv node feeding one activation node; expressing the fusion here lets Inductor lower it to a single conv kernel with a fused pointwise epilogue rather than trying to match already-decomposed clamp primitives at the aten level.
transformation.location: All 6 conv -> hardtanh sites across the three blocks (both depthwise and pointwise stages).
fx_steps: if producer.op != 'call_function' or producer.target != torch.ops.aten.convolution.default: continue
transformation.code_hint: Rely on Inductor pointwise epilogue fusion: once BN is folded (OPT-1) and hardtanh is the conv's sole consumer, the default scheduler fuses the clamp into the conv kernel. The pass mainly ensures the conv output is not forced to materialize (no intervening view/clone with multiple users).

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes the residual standalone clamp read+write that survives OPT-1, especially on the depthwise stages; each fused epilogue saves one activation round-trip per conv site.

optimizations.notes: Depends on OPT-1: only after BN is folded does conv->hardtanh become a clean single-producer/single-consumer pair Inductor will epilogue-fuse. Running before OPT-1 leaves the BN affine kernel in between and blocks fusion. Medium confidence because realized saving depends on the scheduler choosing epilogue fusion; Triton-lowered depthwise convs fuse readily, but cuDNN/cutlass-backed convs may not accept a Triton epilogue and would still need a separate clamp kernel.

global_notes: 0 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 0% of attributed time); all listed operators are >= 1.25%. The large 'layer::unique::prologue' NVTX aggregate (33 kernels, 442528 ns) was EXCLUDED from the time budget because it is a fused superset NVTX range that double-counts the same kernels attributed individually to the aten conv / BN / convolution operators (fused_kernel_double_count). Including it would roughly double the budget.

total_attributed_ns = 387647 (sum over the distinct, non-overlapping aten operators with non-null aggregated, excluding the prologue superset). duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu counter-collection overhead and are used only for relative comparison within this profile.

Architecture: device_name 'NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition' was not present in a hardware-limits knowledge file at the expected path; treated as Blackwell-class. warp_cycles_per_instruction is null for every kernel (removed on Blackwell as expected), so eligible_cycles_pct < 20 was used as the latency-bound indicator. ASSUMPTION: exact sm_count and ridge point for this part were unavailable, so Waves/SM was not computed numerically; the wave-starvation reading for the cutlass GEMMs rests on achieved_occupancy ~8% plus the small 784-block grids, which holds regardless of exact SM count.

edge_case_flag warm_up_inflation: per-op kernel_counts are 1/2/6/12 and the prologue range holds 33 kernels mixing first-iteration and steady-state launches; capture_metadata has no measure_iters field, so per-iteration normalization could not be verified. Relative percentages are unaffected.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 (aten conv-BN fold) -> enables OPT-3 (functional conv+ReLU6 epilogue fusion). OPT-2 (aten channels_last) is independent. The backend funnel runs functional->aten->inductor_config; OPT-3's clean conv->hardtanh pattern only exists once BN is folded, so the BN-fold rewrite must be sequenced before the epilogue-fusion pass in the pipeline. No cycles.

No dtype-promotion pass proposed: the workload is FP32 and the cutlass GEMMs already engage tensor cores (s1688 path). A bf16 promotion would change numerics and was not requested, but is the most promising NEXT step to lift tensor_core_active_pct on the pointwise GEMMs if accuracy tolerance allows.
unattributed_kernels is empty; the 19 init/warmup kernels (k_00000-k_00018) are correctly flagged as initialization kernels in warnings[] and were excluded from attribution.
